home.hero_subtitle: A four-year degree program preparing highly competent, ethical, and research-capable teachers of mathematics and science for Philippine secondary schools.
home.intro_title: Educating the Educators of Science and Mathematics
home.intro_body: The BS Mathematics and Science Teaching (BSMST) program combines rigorous training in mathematics and natural science content with a strong foundation in pedagogy, educational psychology, and field-based teaching practice, producing graduates who are ready to lead classrooms and communities.
home.pillar1_body: Deep coursework in mathematics, biology, chemistry, and physics ensures graduates can teach with mastery and confidence.
home.pillar2_body: Courses in educational theory, assessment, and curriculum design are grounded in current research on how students learn.
home.pillar3_body: Supervised practice teaching in partner schools develops classroom-ready skills before graduation.
home.highlight1_body: Curriculum aligned with the Licensure Examination for Teachers (LET) content and pedagogy domains.
home.highlight2_body: Learn from faculty with graduate degrees in science, mathematics, and science education.
home.highlight3_body: Access to CAS teaching laboratories and instructional technology facilities.
home.highlight4_body: Outreach programs bring science and mathematics literacy to nearby public schools.
home.callout_title: Ready to shape the next generation of learners?
home.callout_body: Admissions for incoming freshmen and shiftees are now open. Learn about requirements and deadlines.

about.title: About BS Mathematics and Science Teaching
about.intro: The program is offered by the College of Arts and Sciences of the University of the Philippines Los Baños, drawing on the College's strength in the natural sciences and mathematics to prepare well-rounded secondary school teachers.
about.mission_body: To produce competent, innovative, and socially responsible teachers of mathematics and science who can address the learning needs of Filipino secondary students.
about.vision_body: To be a recognized center of excellence in mathematics and science teacher education in the Philippines and the Southeast Asian region.
about.objectives_intro: Graduates of the BSMST program are expected to:
about.objective1: Demonstrate mastery of core concepts in mathematics and the natural sciences.
about.objective2: Apply research-based pedagogical strategies suited to diverse learners.
about.objective3: Design and implement standards-based instructional materials and assessments.
about.objective4: Engage in reflective practice and continuing professional development.
about.objective5: Uphold professional and ethical standards of the teaching profession.
about.objective6: Contribute to community and national development through science and mathematics literacy.
about.history_body: The BS Mathematics and Science Teaching program was established by the College of Arts and Sciences to respond to the national need for qualified secondary school mathematics and science teachers, combining the College's disciplinal expertise with professional education coursework.

curriculum.title: Curriculum
curriculum.intro: The BSMST curriculum totals 200+ units across four years, integrating General Education, mathematics and science content courses, professional education courses, and field study / practice teaching.
curriculum.year1_body: College Algebra, Trigonometry, General Chemistry, General Biology, Communication Skills, Philippine History, Foundations of Education, NSTP/PE.
curriculum.year2_body: Calculus I & II, General Physics, Organic Chemistry, Educational Psychology, Curriculum Development, Statistics, Field Study 1.
curriculum.year3_body: Methods of Teaching Mathematics, Methods of Teaching Science, Assessment of Learning, Educational Technology, Research in Science & Math Education, Field Study 2.
curriculum.year4_body: Practice Teaching in partner secondary schools, Special Topics in Science/Math Education, Undergraduate Thesis, Professional Ethics for Teachers.

faculty.title: Faculty
faculty.intro: Our faculty members hold graduate degrees in mathematics, the natural sciences, and science/mathematics education, and are active in research and curriculum development.
faculty.member1_bio: PhD in Science Education. Research interests: inquiry-based learning, teacher professional development.
faculty.member2_bio: MS in Mathematics. Specializes in curriculum design for secondary mathematics education.
faculty.member3_bio: MS in Biology Education. Focuses on laboratory-based science instruction.
faculty.member4_bio: MS in Chemistry. Teaches general and organic chemistry courses for pre-service teachers.
faculty.member5_bio: MS in Physics. Research interests: conceptual change in physics instruction.
faculty.member6_bio: MA in Education. Supervises Field Study and Practice Teaching courses.

admissions.title: Admission Requirements
admissions.intro: Admission to the BSMST program follows University of the Philippines system-wide undergraduate admission policies, with additional program-specific requirements for shiftees and transferees.
admissions.freshmen_body: Apply through the UP College Admission Test (UPCAT). Qualified examinees may indicate UPLB and the BSMST program as a choice during application.
admissions.shiftee_body: Currently enrolled UP students and transferees from other institutions may apply through the CAS shifting/transfer process, subject to slot availability and academic standing requirements.
admissions.doc2_note: From current or last school attended
admissions.doc5_note: Available at the CAS Office of the College Secretary
admissions.callout_body: Reach out to the program office for guidance on requirements and deadlines.

news.title: News & Events
news.intro: Announcements, activities, and milestones from the BSMST program community.
news.item1_body: Fourth-year BSMST students attended orientation ahead of their practice teaching placement in partner secondary schools.
news.item2_body: BSMST students conducted a science and mathematics literacy outreach activity for nearby public high schools.
news.item3_body: Graduating students presented their undergraduate theses on mathematics and science education topics.
